varieties.name: 1. Common/Garden Valerian - Standard Type
varieties.traits: Perennial herb to 1.5m tall; pinnately divided leaves; hollow, grooved stems
varieties.growing_notes: POOR adaptability to humid subtropical climates heat/humidity. Prefers cooler climates (zones 4-9). Will struggle in humid subtropical climates summers. May survive as cool-season annual but likely won't thrive. Requires full sun to part shade, moist soil.
varieties.availability: Limited in humid subtropical climates. The Seed Collection, Eden Seeds, Succeed Heirlooms (Australian suppliers)

varieties.name: 2. Red Valerian (Centranthus ruber / Valeriana rubra) - Standard
varieties.origin: Mediterranean region (Portugal, Spain, France, Italy, Greece, North Africa)
varieties.traits: Multi-stemmed perennial to 80cm; compact, upright growth; blue-green lanceolate leaves
varieties.growing_notes: MODERATE to GOOD adaptability to humid subtropical climates. Mediterranean origin suits warm climates better than V. officinalis. Drought tolerant once established. Heat tolerant. NOT the same as medicinal valerian. Ornamental use only. Can self-seed aggressively - deadhead to control.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Australia) - available in humid subtropical climates subtropical zones

varieties.name: 3. Red Valerian 'Coccineus'
varieties.origin: Cultivated selection from Mediterranean red valerian
varieties.traits: Compact form, 60cm tall; darker red flowers than species; blue-green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: MODERATE to GOOD for humid subtropical climates. Heat and drought tolerant. More compact than standard type. Better for hot climates than European valerian. Ornamental only.

varieties.name: 4. White Valerian (Centranthus ruber 'Albus')
varieties.origin: White-flowered cultivar of Mediterranean red valerian
varieties.traits: Grows 40-60cm tall; compact perennial; grey-green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: MODERATE to GOOD for humid subtropical climates subtropical conditions. Drought and heat tolerant once established. Performs well in alkaline soils. Ornamental use.

varieties.name: 5. Red Valerian 'Rosenrot'
varieties.traits: Grows to 60cm; intense pink/rose flowers; compact habit
varieties.growing_notes: MODERATE for humid subtropical climates. Heat tolerant Mediterranean genetics. Better than V. officinalis for subtropical conditions.

varieties.name: 6. Valerian 'Lubelski' (Polish Cultivar)
varieties.origin: Poland (Lublin region), developed 1950s from wild diploid selections; tetraploid form
varieties.traits: High-yielding thick roots (up to 1kg fresh root/plant); tall stems 1-1.5m; robust growth
varieties.yield_notes: 48-90g dry weight per plant (superior to wild types)
varieties.growing_notes: POOR for humid subtropical climates. Poland's largest commercial cultivar but bred for cool temperate climates. Will suffer in humid subtropical climates heat/humidity. Best for zones 4-7. Available as certified seed but NOT recommended for subtropical cultivation.
varieties.availability: Richters (Canada), Incredible Seeds (Canada) - international import only

varieties.name: 7. Valerian 'Polka' (Polish Cultivar)
varieties.traits: Thin roots; lower yields than 'Lubelski' but higher concentration of active compounds
varieties.growing_notes: POOR for humid subtropical climates. Cool climate cultivar. Will not adapt to subtropical heat.
varieties.availability: Polish gene bank; limited international availability

varieties.name: 8. Valerian 'Norweski' (Norwegian Type)
varieties.traits: Adapted to short northern growing seasons; cold-hardy
varieties.growing_notes: UNSUITABLE for humid subtropical climates. Extreme cold climate adaptation makes it incompatible with subtropical conditions.

varieties.name: 9. Valeriana officinalis subsp. sambucifolia
varieties.traits: Marsh-adapted ecotype; found on coal measures; diploid wild type
varieties.growing_notes: UNSUITABLE for humid subtropical climates. Requires wet, cool conditions and marshy habitat.

varieties.name: 10. Valeriana officinalis subsp. collina
varieties.traits: Drought-adapted ecotype; smaller plant than marsh types
varieties.growing_notes: MARGINAL for humid subtropical climates. Dry-adapted but still requires cool climate. Alkaline soil tolerance might help.

varieties.name: 1. Indian Valerian / Tagar (Valeriana jatamansi)
varieties.origin: Himalayas (India, Nepal, Pakistan, China); 1200-3000m elevation native range
varieties.traits: Perennial aromatic herb to 60cm tall; rhizomatous; erect pubescent stems; deeply divided toothed leaves
varieties.growing_notes: MARGINAL to MODERATE for tropical monsoon climates lowlands. Naturally grows at high elevations (cool mountain habitat). May survive in tropical monsoon climates highland areas (Western Ghats) but lowland tropical heat will stress it. Best cultivated at elevation or in cooler seasons. Prefers part shade, moist well-drained soil. Critical traditional medicine plant - endangered from overharvesting.
varieties.availability: Indian Ayurvedic herb suppliers, traditional medicine markets; cultivation programs in Uttarakhand

varieties.name: 2. Valeriana wallichii (Syn. of V. jatamansi)
varieties.traits: Now considered synonym of V. jatamansi; historically treated as separate species
varieties.growing_notes: Same cultivation requirements as V. jatamansi. MARGINAL for tropical monsoon climates lowlands, MODERATE for highland cultivation.
varieties.availability: Ayurvedic suppliers; commercial substitute for European V. officinalis in India

varieties.name: 3. Valeriana hardwickii
varieties.origin: Eastern Himalayas (Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram); high altitude species
varieties.traits: Perennial herb to 150cm; short rhizomes with slender roots; pinnately divided leaves
varieties.growing_notes: UNSUITABLE for tropical monsoon climates lowlands - requires high elevation cool mountain habitat (similar to V. jatamansi). Recently recorded in Phawngpui National Park. Endangered from habitat loss.
varieties.availability: Very limited; conservation programs in Northeast India

varieties.name: 4. Valeriana hardwickii var. arnottiana
varieties.traits: Regional variety of V. hardwickii; similar growth habit but distinct chemical profile
varieties.growing_notes: UNSUITABLE for tropical monsoon climates. High altitude adaptation only.

varieties.name: 5. Red Valerian (Centranthus ruber) - Standard
varieties.growing_notes: MODERATE for tropical monsoon climates. Mediterranean species better adapted to heat than European V. officinalis but still prefers drier conditions than tropical monsoon climates monsoon. May succeed in drier winter season (Dec-Feb). Ornamental only, not medicinal. Listed as invasive in South Africa due to aggressive self-seeding.
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers; not traditional to India

varieties.name: 6. European Valerian (Valeriana officinalis) - NOT RECOMMENDED
varieties.growing_notes: UNSUITABLE for tropical monsoon climates. Requires cool temperate climate with distinct seasons. Tropical monsoon year-round heat and extreme monsoon humidity will cause plant failure. The species naturally prefers zones 4-9 with cool-weather growing seasons. Heat stress, fungal diseases, and poor root development expected. Use Indian Himalayan species instead.
